get.summary:
Lists organization resources that are visible to the user and satisfy
the specified filter.
get.description:
This will only return organizations on which the user has the permission
`resourcemanager.organizations.get`

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Optional. The maximum number of organizations to return in the response.
The server can return fewer organizations than requested. If unspecified,
server picks an appropriate default.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Optional. A pagination token returned from a previous call to
`ListOrganizations` that indicates from where listing should continue.

parameters.name: showDeleted
parameters.description:
Optional. Controls whether organizations in the DELETE_REQUESTED state
should be returned. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Optional. An expression for filtering the results of the request.
Filter rules are case insensitive. Some eligible fields for filtering are:

+ `displayName`
+ `directoryCustomerId`
+ `state`
+ `createTime`

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: Optional. Specify how the results should be sorted.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description: Required. The Organization to create.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
Optional. A unique identifier for the organization. If not provided, the
server will generate one. Must consist of lowercase letters, numbers,
and hyphens.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

get.summary: Fetches an organization resource identified by the specified resource name.

patch.summary:
Updates an organization resource. Only the `display_name` and
`annotations` fields can be updated. Use the field mask to specify which
fields to update. Omitting the field mask updates all mutable fields.
patch.description:
The caller must have `resourcemanager.organizations.update` permission
on the specified organization.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description:
Required. The new definition of the organization. The `name` field must
be populated to identify the organization to update.

displayName.description:
Output only. A human-readable string that refers to the organization in the
Pivox Console. This string is set by the server and cannot be
changed. The string will be set to the primary domain (for example,
"acme.com") of the customer that owns the organization.

state.description: Output only. The organization's current lifecycle state.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Organization was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Organization was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Organization was requested for deletion.

purgeTime.description:
Output only. The time at which this organization will be purged.
Approximately 30 days after deletion.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current value
of the Organization resource. This may be sent on update and delete
requests to ensure the client has an up-to-date value before proceeding.

owner.title:
Output only. The resource name of the organization owner.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/users/{user}`

annotations.description:
Optional. Labels associated with this organization.
Labels are key-value pairs that can be used to organize and track resources.

schema.description:
Required. The new definition of the organization. The `name` field must
be populated to identify the organization to update.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

get.summary: Lists custom domains associated with an organization.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Optional. The maximum number of custom domains to return in the response.
The server can return fewer custom domains than requested. If unspecified,
the server picks an appropriate default.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Optional. A pagination token returned from a previous call to
`ListCustomDomains` that indicates from where listing should continue.

post.summary:
Creates a custom domain for an organization. Once created, the domain
enters the `PENDING` state and the response includes the DNS records
that the domain owner must configure. After DNS verification succeeds,
the server automatically provisions a TLS certificate and transitions
the domain to `ACTIVE`.
post.description:
The caller must have `resourcemanager.customDomains.create` permission
on the parent organization.

parameters.name: customDomain
parameters.description: Required. The custom domain to create. The `domain` field must be set.

parameters.name: customDomainId
parameters.description:
Optional. A unique identifier for the custom domain. If not provided,
the server will generate one.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

get.summary: Retrieves a custom domain associated with an organization.

delete.summary:
Deletes a custom domain from an organization, including TLS certificate
revocation and routing removal.
delete.description:
The caller must have `resourcemanager.customDomains.delete` permission
on the parent organization.

parameters.name: etag
parameters.description:
Optional. The etag known to the client for the expected state of the
custom domain. This is to be used for optimistic concurrency.

parameters.name: validateOnly
parameters.description:
Optional. If set to true, the request will only validate the request
without persisting it.

get.summary: Gets the invitation policy for an organization.

patch.summary: Updates the invitation policy for an organization.

parameters.name: invitationPolicy
parameters.description: Required. The invitation policy with updated fields.

disablePublicEmailAddresses.description:
Optional. When true, invitations to public email addresses (gmail.com,
yahoo.com, etc.) are rejected. The server maintains the list of
public email domains.

allowedDomains.description:
Optional. If non-empty, only email addresses from these domains are
allowed. This is the strictest check and takes precedence over other
settings.

disallowedDomains.description:
Optional. Email addresses from these domains are always rejected,
even if they pass other checks.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the policy was last modified.

etag.description: Output only. A checksum for optimistic concurrency control.

schema.title: Required. The invitation policy with updated fields.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Optional. Maximum number of invitations to return.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Optional. Page token from a previous ListInvitations call.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Optional. An AIP-160 filter expression. Supports filtering by
`state`, `email`, `create_time`.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: Optional. Sort order (e.g. "create_time desc").

post.summary: Creates an invitation to join the organization.
post.description:
The server validates the email against the organization's invitation
policy before creating the invitation.

parameters.name: invitation
parameters.description: Required. The invitation to create.

parameters.name: invitationId
parameters.description:
Optional. A server-generated invitation ID. If not provided, the server
will generate one.

get.summary: Gets an invitation by resource name.

delete.summary: Revokes a pending invitation. Only org admins can revoke invitations.

parameters.name: etag
parameters.description: Optional. The etag for optimistic concurrency control.

post.summary:
Accepts a pending invitation. The caller must be authenticated and
their email must match the invitation's email. On success, a user
membership is created in the organization and the specified role is
assigned.

Declines a pending invitation. The caller must be authenticated and
their email must match the invitation's email.

Gets the access control policy for an organization resource. The policy may
be empty if no such policy or resource exists. The `resource` field should
be the organization's resource name, for example: "organizations/123".
post.description:
Authorization requires the IAM permission
`resourcemanager.organizations.getIamPolicy` on the specified organization.

post.summary:
Sets the access control policy on an organization resource. Replaces any
existing policy. The `resource` field should be the organization's resource
name, for example: "organizations/123".
post.description:
Authorization requires the IAM permission
`resourcemanager.organizations.setIamPolicy` on the specified organization.

post.summary:
Returns the permissions that a caller has on the specified organization.
The `resource` field should be the organization's resource name,
for example: "organizations/123".
post.description: There are no permissions required for making this API call.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

metadata.description:
Service-specific metadata associated with the operation.  It typically
contains progress information and common metadata such as create time.
Some services might not provide such metadata.  Any method that returns a
long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

response.description:
The normal, successful response of the operation.  If the original
method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is
`google.protobuf.Empty`.  If the original method is standard
`Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource.  For other
methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx`
is the original method name.  For example, if the original method name
is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is
`TakeSnapshotResponse`.

longrunningOperation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

expression.description:
Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language
syntax.

title.description:
Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing
its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the
expression.

description.description:
Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which
describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.

location.description:
Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error
reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.

typeExpr.description:
Represents a textual expression in the Common Expression Language (CEL)
syntax. CEL is a C-like expression language. The syntax and semantics of CEL
are documented at https://github.com/google/cel-spec.

Example (Comparison):

    title: "Summary size limit"
    description: "Determines if a summary is less than 100 chars"
    expression: "document.summary.size() < 100"

Example (Equality):

    title: "Requestor is owner"
    description: "Determines if requestor is the document owner"
    expression: "document.owner == request.auth.claims.email"

Example (Logic):

    title: "Public documents"
    description: "Determine whether the document should be publicly visible"
    expression: "document.type != 'private' && document.type != 'internal'"

Example (Data Manipulation):

    title: "Notification string"
    description: "Create a notification string with a timestamp."
    expression: "'New message received at ' + string(document.create_time)"

The exact variables and functions that may be referenced within an expression
are determined by the service that evaluates it. See the service
documentation for additional information.

role.description:
Role that is assigned to the list of `members`, or principals.
For example, `roles/viewer`, `roles/editor`, or `roles/owner`.

members.description:
* `allUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is
   on the internet; with or without an account.

* `allAuthenticatedUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone
   who is authenticated with an account or a service account.

* `user:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a specific
   account. For example, `alice@example.com`.

* `serviceAccount:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a service
   account. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com`.

* `group:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a group.
   For example, `admins@example.com`.

* `domain:{domain}`: The domain (primary) that represents all the
   users of that domain. For example, `example.com`.
members.title:
Specifies the principals requesting access for a Pivox resource.
`members` can have the following values:

condition.description:
Optional. The condition that is associated with this binding.

If the condition evaluates to `true`, then this binding applies to the
current request.

If the condition evaluates to `false`, then this binding does not apply to
the current request. However, a different role binding might grant the same
role to one or more of the principals in this binding.

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the custom domain. Format:
`organizations/{organization}/customDomains/{custom_domain}`

domain.description:
Immutable. The fully qualified domain name, for example
`pivox.acme.com`. Cannot be changed after creation — delete and
recreate the resource to use a different domain.

state.description: Output only. The current lifecycle state of the custom domain.

requiredDnsRecords.description:
Output only. The DNS records that the domain owner must create to verify
ownership and route traffic. Typically includes a `TXT` record for
verification and a `CNAME` record for routing.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the custom domain was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the custom domain was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the custom domain was requested for deletion.

verifyTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when DNS verification was last confirmed.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current value
of the CustomDomain resource. This may be sent on delete requests to
ensure the client has an up-to-date value before proceeding.

v1CustomDomain.description:
A custom domain associated with an organization. Custom domains allow
organizations to serve Pivox services under their own domain name
(for example, `pivox.acme.com`). The server handles DNS verification
and TLS certificate provisioning automatically.

v1CustomDomainState.description:
The lifecycle state of a custom domain.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state. This is only useful for distinguishing unset values.
 - PENDING: The domain has been created and is waiting for the owner to configure
the required DNS records.
 - PROVISIONING: The DNS records have been verified. The server is provisioning a TLS
certificate via the ACME protocol.
 - ACTIVE: The domain is fully verified, the TLS certificate has been issued, and
traffic is being served.
 - FAILED: DNS verification or TLS certificate provisioning failed. Inspect the
`required_dns_records` to ensure records are configured correctly.
 - DEACTIVATED: The domain was previously active but has been deactivated because DNS
records were removed or the TLS certificate could not be renewed.

type.description: The DNS record type, for example `CNAME`, `TXT`, or `A`.

host.description:
The hostname to create the record on, for example
`_pivox.acme.com` or `pivox.acme.com`.

value.description:
The value for the DNS record, for example
`pivox-verify=abc123` or `custom.pivox.io`.

v1DnsRecord.description:
A DNS record that a domain owner must configure in their DNS provider
to verify ownership or route traffic.

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the invitation.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/invitations/{invitation}`

email.description: Required. The email address of the user being invited.

role.title:
Optional. The role to assign when the invitation is accepted.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/roles/{role}`

state.description: Output only. The current state of the invitation.

inviter.description: Output only. The resource name of the user who created the invitation.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the invitation was created.

expireTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the invitation expires.

acceptTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the invitation was accepted.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current value
of the Invitation resource.

v1Invitation.description:
An invitation to join an organization. Invitations are created by org
admins and sent to users by email. The recipient can accept or decline.

name.title:
Output only. The resource name of the invitation policy.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/invitationPolicy`

disablePublicEmailAddresses.description:
Optional. When true, invitations to public email addresses (gmail.com,
yahoo.com, etc.) are rejected. The server maintains the list of
public email domains.

allowedDomains.description:
Optional. If non-empty, only email addresses from these domains are
allowed. This is the strictest check and takes precedence over other
settings.

disallowedDomains.description:
Optional. Email addresses from these domains are always rejected,
even if they pass other checks.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the policy was last modified.

etag.description: Output only. A checksum for optimistic concurrency control.

v1InvitationPolicy.description:
The invitation policy for an organization. Controls which email
addresses can be invited to join the organization.

v1InvitationState.description:
Invitation lifecycle states.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state.
 - PENDING: The invitation is pending acceptance.
 - ACCEPTED: The invitation has been accepted and a user membership was created.
 - DECLINED: The invitation was declined by the recipient.
 - REVOKED: The invitation was revoked by an org admin.
 - EXPIRED: The invitation expired before being accepted or declined.

customDomains.description: The list of custom domains for the specified organization.

nextPageToken.description:
A pagination token to be used to retrieve the next page of results.
If empty, this response contains the last page of results.

v1ListCustomDomainsResponse.description:
The response returned from the
[ListCustomDomains][pivox.api.v1.Organizations.ListCustomDomains]
method.

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page of results.

organizations.description:
The list of Organizations that matched the list request, possibly
paginated.

nextPageToken.description:
A pagination token to be used to retrieve the next page of results. If the
result is too large to fit within the page size specified in the request,
this field will be set with a token that can be used to fetch the next page
of results. If this field is empty, it indicates that this response
contains the last page of results.

v1ListOrganizationsResponse.description: The response returned from the `ListOrganizations` method.

name.description:
Output only. The resource name of the organization. This is the
organization's relative path in the API. Its format is
"organizations/[organization_id]". For example, "organizations/1234".

displayName.description:
Output only. A human-readable string that refers to the organization in the
Pivox Console. This string is set by the server and cannot be
changed. The string will be set to the primary domain (for example,
"acme.com") of the customer that owns the organization.

state.description: Output only. The organization's current lifecycle state.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Organization was created.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Organization was last modified.

deleteTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the Organization was requested for deletion.

purgeTime.description:
Output only. The time at which this organization will be purged.
Approximately 30 days after deletion.

etag.description:
Output only. A checksum computed by the server based on the current value
of the Organization resource. This may be sent on update and delete
requests to ensure the client has an up-to-date value before proceeding.

owner.title:
Output only. The resource name of the organization owner.
Format: `organizations/{organization}/users/{user}`

annotations.description:
Optional. Labels associated with this organization.
Labels are key-value pairs that can be used to organize and track resources.

v1Organization.description:
The root node in the resource hierarchy to which a particular entity's
(a company, for example) resources belong.

v1OrganizationState.description:
Organization lifecycle states.

 - STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state.  This is only useful for distinguishing unset values.
 - ACTIVE: The normal and active state.
 - DELETE_REQUESTED: The organization has been marked for deletion by the user.

policy.description:
REQUIRED: The complete policy to be applied to the `resource`. The size of
the policy is limited to a few 10s of KB. An empty policy is a
valid policy but certain Pivox services (such as Projects)
might reject them.

updateMask.title:
OPTIONAL: A FieldMask specifying which fields of the policy to modify. Only
the fields in the mask will be modified. If no mask is provided, the
following default mask is used:

permissions.description:
The set of permissions to check for the `resource`. Permissions with
wildcards (such as '*' or 'storage.*') are not allowed. For more
information see
[IAM Overview](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/overview#permissions).

bindings.description:
Associates a list of `members`, or principals, with a `role`. Optionally,
may specify a `condition` that determines how and when the `bindings` are
applied. Each of the `bindings` must contain at least one principal.

etag.description:
`etag` is used for optimistic concurrency control as a way to help
prevent simultaneous updates of a policy from overwriting each other.
It is strongly suggested that systems make use of the `etag` in the
read-modify-write cycle to perform policy updates in order to avoid race
conditions: An `etag` is returned in the response to `getIamPolicy`, and
systems are expected to put that etag in the request to `setIamPolicy` to
ensure that their change will be applied to the same version of the policy.

v1Policy.description:
An Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy, which specifies access
controls for Pivox resources.

A `Policy` is a collection of `bindings`. A `binding` binds one or more
`members`, or principals, to a single `role`. Principals can be user
accounts, service accounts, groups, and domains. A `role` is a named list
of permissions; each `role` can be a predefined role or a user-created
custom role.

permissions.description:
A subset of `TestPermissionsRequest.permissions` that the caller is
allowed.
